title: Limnetis juncea
intro:
I couldn't find any information on the plant Limnetis juncea. However, I found that a possible match is Limnophila juncea, also known as Limnetis juncea. 

Limnophila juncea, also known as Limnetis juncea, is a plant species belonging to the family Plantaginaceae. It is also known as Water Hyssop or Limnophila. 

This plant is used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, which include antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Limnophila juncea include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system.

The bioactive constituents of Limnophila juncea include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The bioactive compounds in Limnophila juncea include limnophyllin, limnetin, and flavonoids such as quercetin and kaempferol.

Limnophila juncea is commonly used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures. The most common herbal preparations made from Limnophila juncea include tea, infusion, and tincture.
